---
title: "Activity 2"
format: pdf
editor: visual
author: 
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| warning: FALSE

#Add libraries as needed
library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

#Read in Dataset
insurance <- read.csv("insurance_data_S26")

```

## Introduction/Background
In the intitial exploration
age, children,


initialModel <-










## Data Exploration
In our intitial exploration, we decided to take a quick look at the variables and guessed at which variables have the most effect, similar to what we did in a previous activity. We initially think that age, smoker, children, and prior claims could have the most accuracy on the model.



Initial model:
initial_model <- lm(
  charges ~ priorclaims + + smoker + age + children,
  data = insurance
)
summary(initial_model)



## Modeling Methods/Strategies
## Results
  # A. Initial Results
  #B. Interpretations
  #C. Model Evaluation
  D. Refinements
Final Recommendations
Author Contributions/Responsibilities
Code Appendix
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
